sops.title: Collaborator SOP
sops.description: How humans and agents open compliant PRs against a PLX-tracked repo: the compliance gate, MC-Checkout discipline, audit mirror, and risk-tier bundles.
sops.audience: Anyone who opens PRs against a PLX-tracked repo (by hand or via an agent)

sops.title: Agent PR & MC-Checkout discipline
sops.description: Linking agent work to a Mission Control task: checkout, the MC-Checkout stamp (one per task), the human accountable owner, and the tier bundle the gate expects.
sops.audience: Anyone driving an AI agent (Cursor, Claude Code, ChatGPT/Codex) against a tracked repo

sops.title: Repo hygiene & evidence bundles
sops.description: Where files belong: approved root files, forbidden patterns, dated artifact bundles, and documented archives — derived from the repo hygiene spec.
sops.audience: Contributors and agents adding files, docs, or evidence to a tracked repo

sops.title: Rollback plan requirements
sops.description: What a Rollback Plan must contain per risk tier, when it is required, and how the gate checks for it on non-docs changes.
sops.audience: Anyone opening a PR beyond docs/tests on a tracked repo
